title: Pharmacy and optical dispensing assistants
description: Prepare medications under the direction of a pharmacist. May measure, mix, count out, label, and record amounts and dosages of medications according to prescription orders. Assist ophthalmologists by performing ophthalmic clinical functions. May administer eye exams, administer eye medications, and instruct the patient in care and use of corrective lenses. Perform administrative and certain clinical duties under the direction of a physician. Administrative duties may include scheduling appointments, maintaining medical records, billing, and coding information for insurance purposes. Clinical duties may include taking and recording vital signs and medical histories, preparing patients for examination, drawing blood, and administering medications as directed by physician. (Based on 5 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Change dressings on wounds and clean or sterilize surgical instruments and ophthalmic or other equipment according to prescribed methods.
Assess refractive conditions of eyes using retinoscopes and measure visual acuity, including near, distance, pinhole, or dynamic visual acuity using appropriate tests.

Operate ophthalmic equipment, such as autorefractors, phoropters, and conduct tonometry tests to measure intraocular pressure, conduct visual field tests to measure field of vision, and assist physicians during ophthalmic procedures including surgery.
Explain treatment procedures, medications, and instructions to patients, and instruct patients in the care and use of contact lenses.
Collect blood, tissue, or other laboratory specimens, log the specimens, prepare them for testing, and perform routine laboratory tests and sample analyses.
Prepare and administer medications as directed and prepack bulk medicines, fill bottles with prescribed medications, and affix labels according to written prescriptions.
Interview patients to obtain medical information, measure their vital signs, record their medical histories and vital statistics, and prepare patients for examination.
Operate cash registers to accept payment from customers and compute charges for medication dispensed; assist customers by answering simple questions or locating items.
Maintain proper storage and security conditions for drugs, receive incoming supplies, verify quantities, and keep inventory updated.
Schedule appointments for patients, contact medical facilities to schedule tests or admissions, and greet and log in patients arriving at the office or clinic.
Inventory and order medical, lab, or office supplies and maintain knowledge of current sales and promotions, policies regarding payment, and exchanges.
Assist patients in selecting eyewear, adjust or make minor repairs to spectacles, and help customers with fitting merchandise and answering product-related questions.
Prepare sales slips, bag purchases, and demonstrate the use of merchandise to customers if necessary.
Maintain and clean work and inventory areas and watch for and recognize security risks and thefts.
